Glowing Red Stone  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
AC: 10
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
WIS: +6 INT: +6 HP: +40
WT: 0.0 Size: TINY
Class: ALL except BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)
Glowing Red Stone
Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
AC: 10
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
WIS: +6 INT: +6 HP: +40
WT: 0.0 Size: TINY
Class: ALL except BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Item Type:Shield
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:55 pp 5 gp 5 sp 5 cp
Tribute:1300
Submitted By:Mizy
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-03 04:31:40
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Average Price: 3,000pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 45
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Velketor's Labyrinth
NPC Name
Lord Doljonijiarnimorinar


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Velketor's Labyrinth


Uploaded November 20th, 2022 by halfridge

GRS

# Jan 29 2002 at 12:25 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591773565061
5 posts
Lord Doljonijiarnimorinar drops it. It fits primary or secondary hand. It looks like a blinking red light in your hand when equipped.

▲ Page top
This stone
QuoteReply
# Nov 14 2001 at 1:21 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593966001431
22 posts
i ahd a glowing red stone and a Dracnid Thyxl equipped on my mage. They looks pretty cool. This stone is good for trade skill as everyone can use it. (all all). Not many ppl are selling it on my server though, unlike the glowing black stone.

# Jul 15 2001 at 2:51 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361512842
6 posts
Does this thing relate in any way to the fabled Glowing Black Stone, or GBS, as it is often called? If so, how are they related? I need answers. If anybody has any info, please share.
▲ Page top
RE: Wait a sec...
QuoteReply
# Aug 02 2001 at 4:50 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591761033621
93 posts
Hrm..that was rather stupid..they must look cool when both equipped though heh
▲ Page top
RE: Wait a sec...
QuoteReply
# Aug 11 2001 at 10:07 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593965792734
105 posts
"Lots" of casters have GBS equipped in primary and Dracnid Thyxl in secondary. Similar stats, one red glowing things in each hand. Pretty cool IMHO, it's a fashion thingy. Just please get a robe that goes with the stones :P
▲ Page top
No relation..
QuoteReply
# Aug 02 2001 at 4:49 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Think of it this way..in farm areas (qeynos will be the farm) the ground is black (hence black stone) and in VL its red, kinda like texas..red dirt..hence why this one is red...u get it?
▲ Page top
RE: No relation..
QuoteReply
# Nov 09 2001 at 6:36 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361502777
41 posts
last time i was there the ground was ice-blue.
well except the stones in the castle :p
▲ Page top
This is great for a chanter
QuoteReply
# Jul 14 2001 at 3:50 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361516282
2 posts
Hmm i think a lot of people are missing 1 thing. This is 10AC 50hp in primary! so between this and my ornater rune shield i can get uber ac! ^_^ the int is just fluff, i need the AC/Hp to survive mezz resists. BTW for all you CHA chanters, it makes little to no difference. Once i gave up on 200 cha (i go about 115 unbuffed) and went for 650 ac(unbuffed) and most hp i could get i ended up surviving a lot more. Just my 2 cp
▲ Page top
RCC is better
QuoteReply
# May 25 2001 at 5:53 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Check out the Roykl's Channel Crystal. AC10 +50 Hps and mana. I use this unless I need resists.

anon lvl 59 chanter
▲ Page top
Not to complain
QuoteReply
# Apr 24 2001 at 8:27 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Ummmm it's called a glowing RED stone yet it has an image of a clear one. I hope it is red IG.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 31 2003 at 3:55 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) actually it does glow red, i have one and you don't cause im l33t an yer not =P
Nosferatu
QuoteReply
# Apr 23 2001 at 5:44 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1594570601017
42 posts
Nosferatu you don't have to give up bash. What a lot of smaller race warriors do(who don't have slam) to keep bash is this: Make a hotkey in your inventory of the shield. Make a hotkey of your second hand slot. Whenever bash is up, switch your shield and second hand slot. Then switch back after the bash.
▲ Page top
Enchanters
QuoteReply
# Apr 22 2001 at 9:01 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
IMO, I think this item is actually pretty well suited for Enchanters, until they can attain an Ornate Runed Shield of course. They are about the only casters that REALLY need AC. As for Healers, don't bother. Yes, if this was a ranged Item, I'd throw away that Howling Harpoon in a hearbeat, but alas, it's not. But, I don't really think it's a bad item. And if (and I hope) it looks like a GBS you can be sure there will b eplenty of caster twinks using them so that they look good as well as having good stats.
-Horaxe Dreamwalker<Dreamwalkers>
48th Barb Shaman, Tarrew Marr Server
▲ Page top
RE: Enchanters
QuoteReply
# Apr 23 2001 at 11:56 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
why would you take this over your howler? howler is better 6 wis 40 mana and its range slot item :)

Other than that it's alright, nothing too fansy :)

# Apr 21 2001 at 12:37 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593965894874
40 posts
The classes that will use this item most effectively cannot use shields and/or cannot dual wield. Great for casters of any type. : )
▲ Page top
RE: to bad
QuoteReply
# Apr 21 2001 at 12:42 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965790794
328 posts
Well...can't say for others, but even tho a Paladin is known for it's 1hs/shield combo, I'd put this in my shield slot. It has the a/c (and hell extra HP too) and of course the wis that a Pally likes. I would have to sacrifice bash, but hey...if it has a graphic (in hand) all the better. Nice item off a pretty difficult MOB...not bad VI. IMHO

and before you flame me, I know that a caster would find more use for this...was just saying that a Pally wouldn't mind this at all...and look, it's not NO DROP...:)

If you're a paladin, do the silent watch shield quest, and you can use a shield with 10 wis, 10 ac, that can go in the back slot (best place for it). Questable in, hmm, mid-thirties, I think.
